title: EQ Mixing Fundamentals
description: Learn to blend two tracks by swapping frequency bands — the foundation of smooth DJ transitions.

requiredTracks.characteristics: tracks with distinct bass patterns, house or techno recommended

steps.instruction: Watch: both decks are playing together at matched BPMs. Listen to the combined sound.
Watch: Deck A's bass is cut completely. Notice how Deck B's bass takes over the low end.
Watch: the highs swap — Deck A's highs cut, Deck B's highs come through.

phases.instruction: Think of the frequency spectrum as layers — bass, mids, highs. You can give each layer to whichever deck you want. That is the power of EQ mixing.

steps.instruction: Make sure both tracks are playing and beatmatched. Listen to the combined sound for a moment.
Cut Deck A's bass completely. Turn the LOW EQ knob all the way down to -24 dB.
Hear the difference? Deck B's bass is now driving the low end by itself. This avoids two basslines fighting each other.
Bring Deck A's bass back to center (0 dB). Turn the LOW knob back to the middle.
Now cut Deck A's highs. Turn the HIGH EQ knob all the way down to -24 dB. Notice how the hats and cymbals disappear.
Swap the highs! Keep Deck A's highs cut and make sure Deck B's highs are at center (0 dB). Deck B now owns the top end.
Practice the bass swap at a phrase boundary. Wait for the phrase to end, then quickly cut Deck A's bass and leave Deck B's bass at 0 dB.
Full frequency swap! Cut both Deck A's bass AND highs. Deck B should have both at center. You have handed the entire mix to Deck B.
